rulings.Case Title: JODIE COLLINS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Gosney¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed through ¿take-home¿ exposure from his father to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Gosney¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JODIE COLLINS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Performance Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed July 20. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JODIE COLLINS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Boss Manufacturing Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and strict liability and taken off calendar as to false representation and punitive damages. As to negligence and strict liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and his deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing gloves and blankets sold or supplied by defendant. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any exposure of plaintiff to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related illness. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JODIE COLLINS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Bragg Investment Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to premises owner/contractor liability and off calendar as to punitive damages. As to premises owner/contractor liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence demonstrating that defendant owned, operated, leased, managed or controlled the premises at which plaintiff alleges he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar due to its failure to demonstrate that plaintiff provided factually devoid responses to written contention discovery regarding plaintiff¿s premises owner/contractor liability claim. Plaintiff dismissed his request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON MCCLARY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff has not presented any admissible evidence that defendant knew or should have known that defendant¿s disturbance of asbestos-containing materials created a safety hazard for a member of Billy Joe McClary¿s household. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Dr. Richard Cohen are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES NASH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 22, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6. Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 3, 4 and 5 (false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). As to negligence and strict liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and his deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s special interrogatory responses identified and discussed various documents supporting plaintiff¿s contention that he was exposed to Merlex asbestos-containing stucco, but defendant failed to address those documents in its moving papers. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff¿s declaration and Exhibit 2 to Mr. Ay¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to Merlex asbestos-containing stucco. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and to Exhibit 2 to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for false representation and civil battery and his request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)
